title: HVAC Setback Mode
text: Setback mode reduces HVAC energy usage by allowing temperature to drift when the building is unoccupied. Typical approach: raise cooling setpoint and lower heating setpoint during off-hours. This can save 10-15% on energy bills annually. Modern systems use occupancy sensors to switch between comfort and setback modes automatically.

title: Zoned HVAC Systems
text: Zoned HVAC divides a building into independent climate zones, each with its own thermostat and damper. Benefits include personalized comfort, reduced energy waste, and the ability to condition only occupied areas. Common zone types: perimeter zones (near windows), interior zones, and server/equipment rooms with dedicated cooling.
